page_content:
### **Email Template: Dr. Kiko Kawamura**
*(The Precise & Principled Academic)*  
Subject: Formal Request for Graduate School Letter of Recommendation – [Your Name]  
Dear Dr. Kawamura,  
I’m writing to formally request your letter of recommendation for my graduate school application in [specific field]. As you remember from our previous correspondence, I was a student in your Applied Statistics and Probability course during the [Semester/Year], where I earned an A grade and gained significant insight into data-driven decision-making.  
To ensure we maintain clear expectations, I’d like to confirm that this letter would be for graduate school purposes only (not for professional roles). As previously discussed, if possible, I’d appreciate it if you could provide the final version by [specific date], as this aligns with my application timeline and submission requirements.  
I’ve attached a brief summary of my recent work in [field] to help contextualize what I’m seeking in your letter (see attachment: "Summary of Recent Work"). Please let me know if any additional details or documentation would be helpful for you to include.  
Thank you again for your consistent support and professional guidance throughout my academic journey.  
Sincerely,
[Your Full Name]
[Student ID/Program, if applicable]
[Contact Information]  
---
